summary: Finished matching Captain Falcon's ftCa_SpecialLw translation unit by making a small source-level codegen adjustment in ftCa_SpecialAirLwEnd_Phys, deleting the remaining hand-written assembly file, and switching the build configuration to compile the C source as Matching.

key_files.role: Matched source file; adjusted ftCa_SpecialAirLwEnd_Phys to use an explicit ftCo_DatAttrs pointer from getFtAttrs(fp).
Deleted 854-line assembly fallback after the C source matched.
Changed ftCa_SpecialLw.c from NonMatching to Matching in the runtime object list.
Switched the object list from the asm object to the C object for ftCa_SpecialLw.

what_changed: Removed asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftCa_SpecialLw.s entirely, including assembly versions of the ftCa_SpecialLw, ftCa_SpecialAirLw, ending, physics, collision, and related local helper routines plus sdata2 constants.
In ftCa_SpecialAirLwEnd_Phys, replaced an unused padding declaration `u8 _[8];` with explicit local pointers `ftCo_DatAttrs* ca;` and `ftCaptain_DatAttrs* da;`.
Changed `ftCaptain_DatAttrs* da = fp->dat_attrs;` into a declaration followed by assignment, preserving the same data source while affecting local/register/stack code generation.
Changed the traction calculation from `da->speciallw_air_landing_traction * fp->co_attrs.gr_friction` to `da->speciallw_air_landing_traction * ca->gr_friction`.
Changed obj_files.mk to build `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftCa_SpecialLw.c.o` instead of `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftCa_SpecialLw.s.o`.
smart_moves: The matching fix was minimal: only one function in the C file changed, while the rest of the PR was the standard asm deletion and build-list flip.
Using `getFtAttrs(fp)` and a separate `ftCo_DatAttrs* ca` avoided direct access through `fp->co_attrs.gr_friction`, which likely produced the desired load sequence for matching the original code.

The source kept the semantic meaning of the traction expression while changing how common fighter attributes were reached, a typical decomp tactic for matching pointer/register behavior without changing behavior.
The PR completed the cleanup loop by removing the asm fallback and updating both configure.py and obj_files.mk, preventing the matched C from being bypassed by the old assembly object.
decomp_lessons: When a function is almost matching but direct struct-member access differs from the target, try introducing the same accessor/helper path used elsewhere, such as `getFtAttrs(fp)`, to influence codegen.
Replacing artificial stack padding with real typed locals can be enough to match register allocation or stack layout while improving source readability.
For finished matching PRs, remember the full conversion checklist: delete the asm file, mark the C object as Matching in configure.py, and update obj_files.mk from asm object to src object.

Captain special-move files may share naming and constants with SpecialHi symbols in the assembly dump; do not assume all `ftCa_SpecialHi_*` names in the deleted asm imply the file being worked on is SpecialHi.
naming_conventions: Captain Falcon down-special functions use the `ftCa_SpecialLw` and `ftCa_SpecialAirLw` prefixes.
Function suffixes follow the fighter action callback convention: `_Enter`, `_Anim`, `_Phys`, and `_Coll`.
Captain-specific data attributes use `ftCaptain_DatAttrs* da`; common fighter data attributes use `ftCo_DatAttrs* ca` in this fix.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use a typed local `ftCo_DatAttrs* ca` and assign it with `getFtAttrs(fp)` before reading `gr_friction` instead of accessing `fp->co_attrs.gr_friction` directly.
Split declaration and assignment of local pointers when matching requires a different codegen pattern than declaration-with-initializer.
Remove dummy padding only when replaced by real locals that reproduce the needed layout or register pressure.
After source matches, replace the asm object in obj_files.mk and switch configure.py from `Object(NonMatching, ...)` to `Object(Matching, ...)`.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.interpretation: Positive feedback only; no technical review guidance was recorded in the available slice.

follow_up_queries: Find other fighter files where direct `fp->co_attrs` access might need to become `getFtAttrs(fp)` for matching.
Search for remaining `u8 _[...]` padding locals in matched-adjacent fighter code that could be replaced by typed locals.
Check other ftCaptain special move files for shared patterns in `_Phys` landing traction calculations.
Verify whether other PRs completing fighter files followed the same configure.py and obj_files.mk conversion checklist.
